operating_state: Total revenue increased 5% to $95.4 billion in Q2 2025, driven by iPhone Pro model strength and 12% Services growth from advertising, App Store, and cloud services. Regional performance was broadly positive with double-digit growth in Japan and Americas, while Greater China declined 2% due to iPhone weakness. Mac and iPad showed strong growth at 7% and 15% respectively, while Wearables declined 5%.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded to 47.1% from 46.6% year-over-year, with Services margin improving to 75.7% from favorable mix while Products margin compressed to 35.9% from unfavorable product mix and currency headwinds. Operating expenses increased to 16% of revenue driven by higher R&D headcount and infrastructure costs plus increased professional services and variable selling expenses.
material_events: Company repurchased $25.0 billion in shares during Q2 and announced additional $100 billion buyback authorization in May 2025. Quarterly dividend increased from $0.25 to $0.26 per share beginning Q3 2025. Released €14.2 billion from escrow to Ireland, fully settling the State Aid Decision obligation. Launched iPhone 16e, updated iPad Air, iPad, MacBook Air, and Mac Studio during the quarter.
macro_exposures: New U.S. tariffs implemented in Q2 2025 targeting imports from China, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Vietnam, and EU with reciprocal retaliatory measures imposed by affected countries. Department of Commerce initiated Section 232 investigation into semiconductor imports and downstream products. Currency weakness across major markets created unfavorable revenue translation impacts throughout the quarter.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued quarterly volatility in gross margins due to product mix shifts, currency fluctuations, and tariff impacts. Company maintains commitment to annual dividend increases subject to board approval. Manufacturing purchase obligations total $38.4 billion payable within 12 months as of quarter-end.
uncertainty_profile: Tariff impact magnitude remains uncertain pending additional sector-based measures and duration of trade disputes. New accounting standards for expense disaggregation effective Q4 2028 and enhanced segment disclosures effective Q4 2025. Ongoing exposure to supply chain disruptions from geopolitical tensions and natural disasters affecting concentrated manufacturing regions.
